GitHub Support North America

messages.author: Ellie Kelsch
messages.content:
Hello! So I ran the script that James suggested in the previous ticket: https://support.github.com/ticket/personal/0/4321305 (sorry was raised under GHES account). However we are now seeing issues with IdP groups being "disabled" since running these commands. We have a manual workaround of deleting the push group in Okta (but keeping the group in the target app) and then recreating the push group manually but this is not scalable if this issue is affecting all groups, which is roughly 800. Is there any other options here?

Also I would love if this ticket could be assigned back to James as this was a long standing issue that he has all of the context on. Thanks!
Ellie

messages.author: Ellie Kelsch
messages.content: For additional impact context, this is causing previously synced to teams to lose access to GHEC-DR which is very disruptive for those teams at the moment.

messages.author: Ellie Kelsch
messages.content: Sorry I linked the wrong previous ticket, it's here: https://support.github.com/ticket/enterprise/669/4061807

messages.author: GitHub Support (Will)
messages.content:
Hi,

Thanks for writing in. We're currently going over the history and reviewing the ticket. We'll provide next steps shortly.

Regards,
Will

messages.author: James
messages.content:
Hi Ellie,

Thanks for reaching out and sorry to hear you are facing problems here!

I'm just getting to speed on this ticket and I've grabbed it back for now to continue working on it.

Would it be possible to confirm if you are seeing the disabled groups within Okta in this case?

Would it be possible to supply an example IdP group and I'll take a look from my side.

Please let me know if you have any questions and I'll be happy to help!

Kind regards,
James

messages.author: Ellie Kelsch
messages.content:
Hey James! Yes I can see the disabled groups in Okta. Here's some examples of (previously) affected groups that I've had to manually recreate the push groups for to enable the IdP syncing:

- manifesto
- starflock-backstage-external
- temp-access-backstage-external
- ai-fleet-management-backstage-external
- ai-fm-backstage-external

I can try and find some existing groups that are currently disabled and unresolved.

Sorry it's hard to check because some of the groups I deleted via SCIM haven't been linked to GitHub teams yet so they aren't affected.

messages.author: Ellie Kelsch
messages.content: Is there a way to query all disabled IdPs?

messages.content:

Thanks for getting back to me and supplying a few example groups. I'm just digging in to this further my end and I'll update you with my findings shortly!

Please let me know if you have any questions in the meantime and I'll be happy to help.

Kind regards,
James

messages.author: Ellie Kelsch
messages.content:
Hi James,

Thank you so much! Any updates on this?

Just to clarify I can't tell if there are any more groups affected or if it was only groups that were already linked via an IdP to an existing GitHub team (in which case, there may be no more).

Ellie

I'm still working through the logs we have and looking into this on my side, I hope to be finished by the latest tomorrow and will send you over my findings as soon as possible.

I'll keep you updated with my progress and here if you have any questions in the meantime but will update you shortly.

Kind regards,
James

messages.author: Léo
messages.content:
**Server installation**: `ghe.spotify.net` (32416)
**Release**: 3.19

Hello,

Since yesterday afternoon (12pm to 5pm) we've seen very high latency, with saturated unicorn workers. Our mergeability checks are experience extremely high latency too (> 15min to compute mergeability on some PRs).

We have an opened incident on our side to mitigate and try to figure it out. We've already started with some mitigations:
- we've noticed one of our integrations is hitting the LFS endpoints more than usual
- we've noticed an integration doing a very high volume of GET installations calls

However we're not 100% this is the cause, and we'd like help diagnosing it.

I will upload a bundle to this ticket ASAP

messages.author: Olliec
messages.content:
Hi Léo,

Thanks for sharing a bundle and details. We will review and share our findings.

In the meantime let us know if you have any additional questions.

Cheers,
Olliec

messages.author: Olliec
messages.content:
Hi Léo,

Thanks for the bundle. Based on the data, the latency window was driven by a mergeability storm centered on `spotify/services-pilot`, which saturated Unicorn and cascaded into long PR mergeability times.

During 14:01–15:00 UTC on Apr 29, we can see:
- `spotify-ci[bot]` as the top REST API consumer with ~55k requests over ~3 hours of worker time
- `fleet-manager-bot` as the top GraphQL consumer with ~25k requests over ~7 hours of worker time
- `auto_merge_job` running ~7,581 times in a single hour on this repo
- Merge-tree parallelism peaking at 36 concurrent operations, with average parallelism around 14

That execution profile is characteristic of "base-branch fan-out", where frequent pushes to the base branch trigger mergeability re-computation across a large set of open PRs.

At this point, the most useful confirmation from your side is:
1) Which of these automations (`spotify-ci[bot]`, `fleet-manager-bot`, or merge-queue related jobs) is pushing to the base branch of `spotify/services-pilot` during peak hours.
2) Roughly how many open PRs that repository typically has during normal operation.

Those two data points will help confirm the fan-out mechanics and guide whether the most effective mitigation is reducing base-branch push frequency, adjusting merge automation behavior, or managing open PR volume during peak windows.

In the meantime, the fastest stabilizing action if this pattern reappears is to temporarily slow or pause the automation that is pushing to the base branch on this repo, which should immediately reduce mergeability pressure and bring Unicorn utilization back down.

Once mitigated, you should see mergeability checks return to normal completion times and Unicorn utilization drop back to baseline.

Let us know if this helps.

Cheers,
Olliec
